<!--
Thanks for contributing. Keep each PR focused on a single change — open separate
PRs for unrelated work. Fill in the sections that apply and delete the rest.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change? One or two sentences. -->

## Why

<!-- The motivation: the bug, gap, or use case this addresses. A concrete
     scenario is most useful. Link the issue it closes. -->

Closes #

## Type of change

<!-- Keep what applies, delete the rest. -->

- Bug fix
- New feature
- Security hardening
- Documentation
- Refactor or chore (no behavior change)
- Breaking change (call it out and describe the migration path)

## How it was tested

<!-- The commands you ran and what you observed. New behavior needs new tests. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass locally, and new behavior is covered by new tests
- [ ] Docs updated when behavior, configuration, or a public interface changed
- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits and are signed off (`git commit -s`)
- [ ] This PR is a single, focused change
- [ ] The description explains *why*, not just *what*

<!-- Security-relevant change? Spell out the impact: does it affect a trust
     boundary, authorization, secret handling, or fail-closed behavior? Write
     "no security impact" with a reason if none apply. -->
